hints: For `append_log`, open the file with `"a"` mode: `with open(filepath, "a") as f:`. Append mode creates the file if it doesn't exist and adds to the end if it does — never erasing existing content.
Format each entry as an f-string and include the newline: `f.write(f"[LOG] {message}\n")`. Without the `\n`, every entry runs together on the same line.

For `read_log`, check if the file exists first: `if not Path(filepath).exists(): return []`. Then open with `"r"` and build a list. Iterate with `for line in f:`, strip each line, and skip empty ones.
A list comprehension over the file works well: `return [line.strip() for line in f if line.strip()]`. The `if line.strip()` part skips any trailing blank lines.
